jokes.text: I blew mine up, and now it smells like my mouth.
jokes.setup: Asked about neck pillow for travel comfort
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that it smells like her mouth after inflation
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific grossness with efficient delivery, typical Liz oversharing

jokes.text: I never sleep on planes. I don't want to get incepted.
jokes.punchline: Made-up verb from 'Inception' movie about dreams within dreams
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that feels natural yet original, very economical

jokes.text: You're going to Nags Head? Isn't that redundant?
jokes.setup: Liz mentions vacation destination Nags Head
jokes.punchline: Jack implies Liz is a nag, so going to 'Nags Head' is redundant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack-style wordplay with excellent economy and character specificity

jokes.text: You will hand me an envelope predicting my joke about Nags Head.
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary about the predictability of his own humor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that acknowledges the show's own joke patterns while being character-perfect

jokes.text: We're going to Toronto for the G8 Economic Summit. It's going to be... very erotic.
jokes.punchline: Finding an economic summit erotic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy character moment - only he would find economics arousing

jokes.text: I packed underwear that isn't gray!
jokes.punchline: Reveals her usual underwear is gray, implying poor hygiene/care
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific detail about Liz's slovenliness

jokes.text: Like you, Avery is a Type-A nutjob.
jokes.punchline: Casually calling his girlfriend a 'nutjob' while being affectionate
jokes.explanation: Good Jack-style backhanded compliment delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Dating yourself is a double-edged sword.
jokes.setup: Discussing similar personality types in relationships
jokes.punchline: Introduces episode theme while being literally accurate
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode theme efficiently while being character-appropriate wisdom

jokes.text: Well, that would only be a problem if I had any flaws. Not only is your fly open, there's a pencil is sticking out of it.
jokes.setup: Jack claims he has no flaws
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealed to have obvious, absurd wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction with absurdist visual detail. The pencil makes it wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: Good day to you, sir.
jokes.punchline: Maintains formal dignity despite ridiculous situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character beat - maintaining formality in embarrassment

jokes.text: You are more talented than I.
jokes.punchline: Jenna's narcissism makes her unable to give unqualified praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - can't even congratulate someone without making it about herself

jokes.text: You're ready, Mirror Jenna.
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's been rehearsing basic human decency in a mirror
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail - Jenna needs to rehearse being supportive, and has a relationship with her mirror

jokes.text: Clear. I'll need a whale saddle.
jokes.setup: Offered to borrow killer whale for spring break
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact response about needing appropriate equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - accepts absurd premise but focuses on practical details

jokes.text: Kate Capshaw's husband?
jokes.setup: Told Steven Spielberg wants him to star in movie
jokes.punchline: Identifies legendary director only as his actress wife's spouse
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Tracy's unique perspective and knowledge gaps

jokes.text: the guitar-playing chimpanzee that I bought this morning. Dotcom is confirming that he drowned.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentions buying a guitar-playing chimpanzee
jokes.punchline: Casual reveal that it immediately died
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation delivered with perfect Tracy casualness about animal tragedy

jokes.text: Why are you putting me in a suit? I still haven't memorized my Torah passage!
jokes.setup: Kenneth dressing Tracy for formal events
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about Torah passage suggests religious confusion
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy confusion between different formal occasions and religions

jokes.text: Success is a double-edged sword.
jokes.setup: Episode theme about success having drawbacks
jokes.punchline: Kenneth delivers episode title/theme
jokes.explanation: Reinforces episode theme but not particularly innovative

jokes.text: I realize you're more important than everyone else on the flight, what with those Jets sweatpants...
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggests Jets sweatpants indicate importance
jokes.explanation: Good class-based humor with appropriate sarcasm

jokes.text: Excuse me, young lady. Are you old enough to be traveling alone? I don't know. I'm going to visit my Nana, but I'm scared. Wait, that's too young. Roger that.
jokes.setup: Flight attendant checking on unaccompanied minor
jokes.punchline: Treats it like a mission with military-style communication
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on flight attendant power dynamics

jokes.text: This is a $90-million aircraft, not a Tallahassee strip club.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison to Tallahassee strip club
jokes.explanation: Funny specificity - why Tallahassee specifically makes it funnier

jokes.text: And that is Sky Law.
jokes.punchline: Invents official-sounding but ridiculous authority
jokes.explanation: Excellent made-up authority that becomes running gag. Very quotable and absurd.

jokes.text: I'm a businessman. I'm not an air marshal.
jokes.punchline: Overly specific denial of being air marshal
jokes.explanation: Good suspicious denial that makes him seem more suspicious

jokes.text: There's a man on the wing! We haven't taken off yet. It's just a mechanic.
jokes.punchline: Deflated by practical reality
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of classic Twilight Zone reference with practical reality

jokes.text: Oh, God, you're breaking the extra bone all Canadians have in their hands!
jokes.punchline: Made-up Canadian anatomical difference
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd made-up Canadian stereotype

jokes.text: Why did I buy a beryllium mine if her birthstone isn't going to be aquamarine?
jokes.setup: Baby coming early, won't be born in March
jokes.punchline: Jack's over-the-top preparation including buying a mine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy excess - buying a mine for a birthstone is hilariously over-prepared

jokes.text: Good God. She'll be Canadian!
jokes.setup: Realizing baby might be born in Canada
jokes.punchline: Treats Canadian citizenship as horror
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's extreme nationalism

jokes.text: Your milk comes in bags. Bags! Your pavilion at EPCOT doesn't have a ride!
jokes.setup: Canadian asking what's wrong with being Canadian
jokes.punchline: Increasingly petty complaints about Canada
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation from serious to petty complaints, EPCOT reference is particularly absurd

jokes.text: And if Canada is so nice and friendly, why does most of our meth come from your Asian drug gangs?
jokes.punchline: Connects Canadian niceness to drug manufacturing
jokes.explanation: Dark turn in the rant that connects politeness to crime

jokes.text: Are we not even making our own meth? What is happening to American manufacturing?
jokes.punchline: Frames drug production as manufacturing/economic issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Jack treats illegal drugs as legitimate economic concern

jokes.text: If only we were in Kenya right now, we'd be fine.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Obama birther conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Topical political joke but feels more cheap than earned

jokes.text: I'm not scared of you people! And I don't think those cashews look like a bowl of baby penises!
jokes.punchline: Bizarre outburst about cashews looking like baby penises
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd and quotable Tracy outburst at inappropriate venue

jokes.text: And NBC sitcoms that didn't make the schedule.
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about NBC's own failures
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about the network airing the show

jokes.text: to control the herds of walking mozzarella sticks who think that $300 and a photo ID gives them the right to fly through the air like one of the Guardian Owls of legend!
jokes.setup: Explaining why they lie to passengers
jokes.punchline: Dehumanizing passengers as 'walking mozzarella sticks' + owl movie callback
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with creative insult and callback to in-flight movie

jokes.text: Well, good luck pressing 'Take Off', then 'Autopilot', then 'Land'.
jokes.setup: Sarcastically suggesting Liz fly the plane
jokes.punchline: Reduces piloting to three simple button presses
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of piloting complexity while being sarcastic

jokes.text: When I asked the lady at the desk, she told me in kilometers!
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out distance to border
jokes.punchline: Metric system as foreign obstacle
jokes.explanation: Good character moment about Jack's American-centrism but fairly predictable

jokes.text: We ruined those kids' field day.
jokes.punchline: Reveals they dominated children's event
jokes.explanation: Funny reveal that their competitiveness hurt children

jokes.text: To what, Jack? Be a mouthpiece for Federalism and then die in a duel against someone named Aaron? Is that what you want our daughter to be? A big government duel loser?
jokes.setup: Jack mentions Alexander Hamilton as example
jokes.punchline: Reduces Founding Father to 'big government duel loser'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reduction of historical figure to absurd political insult. Perfectly Avery.

jokes.text: Dear God, they've re-started the failed NBC shows. Oh, no. Please, no. Not 'Gals on the Town' again!
jokes.punchline: NBC mocking its own failed shows
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of meta-joke about NBC's failures

jokes.text: Love and friendship, having it all / Or maybe just a great pair of shoes / One of them has to be Asian / Can they be good at their jobs?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and problematic TV tropes
jokes.explanation: Sharp parody of formulaic sitcom diversity and tropes

jokes.text: We were like them once, and we can be again! But we're just airplane folk now!
jokes.punchline: Creates tribal identity around airplane imprisonment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of airplane situation into existential crisis

jokes.text: He compromises readily on movie choices and... sexual positions.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing about their sex life
jokes.explanation: Good Liz oversharing moment with perfect structure

jokes.text: I'm assuming you don't want to stop for gas in the middle of the ocean.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he can't turn on air conditioning
jokes.punchline: Suggests absurd mid-ocean gas station stop
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic applied to airplane operations

jokes.text: Stewart did not study dance at Carnegie Mellon to become a flight attendant to clean bathrooms.
jokes.punchline: Flight attendant's artistic background makes cleaning beneath him
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about flight attendant's artistic aspirations

jokes.text: Those potato chip bags are designed to be opened in flight. You open them at sea level, somebody could be killed.
jokes.punchline: Absurd safety concern about chip bag pressure
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation of minor airline procedures to deadly consequences

jokes.text: If I can't poop on the street, why should my tax dollars pay for someone else to?
jokes.setup: Carol mentioning Liz yelling at police horse
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic about police horse bathroom privileges vs taxes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Liz logic connecting public defecation to taxation

jokes.text: including any lipless middle-aged women in lesbian clown shirts, should please be seated at this time.
jokes.punchline: Specific insult clearly targeting Liz
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of professional announcement to personal attack

jokes.text: Sorry, but Tracy Jordan doesn't do safety schools!
jokes.punchline: Dismisses Ivy League school as 'safety school'
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy arrogance - dismissing Cornell shows his disconnection

jokes.text: Sorry, but Tracy Jordan doesn't do safety schools!
jokes.punchline: Treating Ivy League Cornell as a 'safety school'
jokes.explanation: Tracy's complete ignorance of Cornell's prestige

jokes.text: You ever hear of the 'Peter Principle'? Yes. Just now.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asks if Tracy knows business concept
jokes.punchline: Tracy admits he just learned it this second
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy honesty about his ignorance, great economy

jokes.text: But my incompetence knows no bounds.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Peter Principle about rising to level of incompetence
jokes.punchline: Tracy claims unlimited incompetence, missing the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - turns business principle into personal boast

jokes.text: On D-Day, my grandfather wore a German uniform under his American one, just in case.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his family are famous cowards
jokes.punchline: Grandfather hedged bets during D-Day invasion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark family history that perfectly explains Kenneth's character

jokes.text: Or perform his husband bedroom duties... for instance, painting the bedroom?
jokes.setup: Asking how Kenneth would look his husband in the eye
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'bedroom duties' means literal bedroom maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts sexual expectation with innocent reality

jokes.text: because Al Sharpton doesn't have an office.
jokes.setup: Al Sharpton would denounce Kenneth at street press conference
jokes.punchline: Explains it would be on street because he has no office
jokes.explanation: Good throwaway detail that adds absurd specificity

jokes.text: And I know, because I've looked it up for you a dozen times!
jokes.setup: Telling Tracy he doesn't know meaning of 'scared'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's literally looked up definitions for Tracy before
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth dedication to Tracy, reveals their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Great. I'm Lorne. If you're a cop, you have to tell me.
jokes.punchline: Believes urban myth about police disclosure requirements
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing criminal naivety

jokes.text: I'm still in Girl Scouts. I have 9,000 badges.
jokes.setup: Avery claiming she never quits anything
jokes.punchline: Still in Girl Scouts with impossible number of badges
jokes.explanation: Good Avery overachievement with absurd escalation

jokes.text: Hey, I'm still looking for a golf ball I shanked in 1987. I am not taking that penalty stroke!
jokes.punchline: Decades-long search for golf ball to avoid penalty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack stubbornness with absurd dedication to golf rules

jokes.text: Austin Powers on 'Crossfire', timely stuff.
jokes.punchline: References dated comedy bit on dead political show
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about show's own dated comedy references

jokes.text: "Uh, folks, half an hour means forever."
jokes.punchline: Exaggerates his announcements to reveal truth
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier lies about timing

jokes.text: The card was wrong!
jokes.punchline: Liz still insists she was right despite official answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz stubbornness - can't admit she's wrong even about trivia

jokes.text: I did not! I wanted the books to slide off!
jokes.setup: Liz saying Carol built bookshelf wrong
jokes.punchline: Carol claims sliding books were intentional design
jokes.explanation: Good matching stubbornness - Carol equally unable to admit error

jokes.text: Admit that I, as Captain of this vessel, was in the right. You were wrong to question me, and I was correct in my treatment of you and your fellow animals.
jokes.punchline: Insists on formal captain language and calls passengers animals
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Carol's power trip and dehumanization

jokes.text: Because they sound very sitcomy.
jokes.setup: Questioning if Avery's contractions are real
jokes.punchline: Character notes they sound like TV trope
jokes.explanation: Great meta-moment where character recognizes sitcom conventions

jokes.text: and declare war on Germany, like back when we were awesome.
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic for when America fought Germany
jokes.explanation: Good Jack nationalism with nostalgic wartime reference

jokes.text: They made me memorize all 700 words in the Canadian dictionary.
jokes.punchline: Implies Canadian dictionary is very short
jokes.explanation: Good Canadian stereotype with specific number making it funnier

jokes.text: I know, I'm a stereotype. 'All guys from Quebec are good at karate.'
jokes.punchline: Claims karate skill as Quebec stereotype
jokes.explanation: Brilliant made-up stereotype that sounds plausible but is completely absurd

jokes.text: Oh, my God, where are my manners? Do you want to try meth?
jokes.punchline: Politely offers illegal drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect collision of Canadian politeness with drug dealing

jokes.text: Actually, you dial 272 here.
jokes.punchline: Canada has different emergency number
jokes.explanation: Mild cultural difference joke, not particularly strong

jokes.text: IKEA on a Saturday?
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking about worst place ever
jokes.punchline: Tracy suggests IKEA on weekend as worse than Africa
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy perspective - privileged view of 'worst places'

jokes.text: Having seen 'Crimson Tide' on Showtime last weekend, I believe the only course of action available to us is passenger mutiny! I am Denzel!
jokes.punchline: Uses submarine movie as basis for airplane mutiny
jokes.explanation: Good Liz escalation with specific pop culture reference

jokes.text: I am invoking Sky Law. You are now silenced, shrieking harpy.
jokes.punchline: Invokes 'Sky Law' again with medieval insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Sky Law with escalating authority and archaic insult

jokes.text: I'll be a folk hero, like that guy everybody hates now!
jokes.setup: Liz threatening to deploy emergency slide
jokes.punchline: References hero who became villain (likely Captain Sully)
jokes.explanation: Decent reference to aviation hero's fall from grace

jokes.text: You'll have to go through this old bastard first!
jokes.punchline: Elderly passenger joins fight with self-deprecating battle cry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with elderly passenger self-aware about age

jokes.text: But I'm going to treat her just like a human baby.
jokes.punchline: Jack will treat Canadian baby 'like' human
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Jack's anti-Canadian prejudice

jokes.text: of that double-edged sword just swinging around, trying to cut your faces off.
jokes.punchline: Escalates metaphor to violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of episode metaphor to absurd violence

jokes.text: Oh, no, you don't! We will not be party to this socialist perversion. You will take our money!
jokes.punchline: Demands to pay as matter of principle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - so capitalist he refuses free healthcare

jokes.text: This morning I taught the local schoolchildren all the words to 'Monster Mash'.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his humanitarian work in Africa
jokes.punchline: Teaching novelty song instead of practical skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy charitable work - well-meaning but completely useless

jokes.text: Like how an ant is much bigger than a smaller bug?
jokes.punchline: Uses terrible analogy that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic failure - trying to be profound but missing completely
